ddls:
CREATE TABLE "commands" (
  "command_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each command */,
  "discriminator" TEXT /* Type of command (e.g., MacroCommand, SimpleCommand) */,
  "command_inverse_id" TEXT /* Reference to the inverse command, if applicable */,
  "command_type" TEXT /* Type of command action (e.g., Update, Edit) */,
  "object_type" TEXT /* Type of object this command operates on (e.g., Simulation) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the command */,
  "extended_description" TEXT /* Extended details about the command */,
  "visible" INTEGER /* Indicates if the command is visible in the UI */,
  "comment" TEXT /* Additional comments regarding the command */,
  "sequence" INTEGER /* Sequence number for ordering commands */,
  "parent_id" TEXT /* Reference to the parent command if nesting is used */,
  "created_by" TEXT /* User who created the command */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the command was created */,
  "updated_by" TEXT /* User who last updated the command */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the command was last updated */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the command (e.g., active, deleted) */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version number for the command */,
  "simulation_id" TEXT /* Reference to the simulation this command is associated with */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("command_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_commands_updated_by FOREIGN KEY ("updated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_commands_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_commands_command_inverse_id FOREIGN KEY ("command_inverse_id") REFERENCES commands ("command_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_commands_parent_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_id") REFERENCES commands ("command_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_commands_command_type FOREIGN KEY ("command_type") REFERENCES command_types ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_commands_object_type FOREIGN KEY ("object_type") REFERENCES object_types ("object_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_commands_simulation_id FOREIGN KEY ("simulation_id") REFERENCES simulations ("simulation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "simulations" (
  "simulation_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each simulation */,
  "simulation_name" TEXT /* Name of the simulation */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the simulation */,
  "date_created" TEXT /* Date the simulation was created */,
  "created_by" TEXT /* User who created the simulation */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the simulation was created */,
  "updated_by" TEXT /* User who last updated the simulation */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the simulation was last updated */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the simulation (e.g., active, paused, completed) */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version number for the simulation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("simulation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_simulations_updated_by FOREIGN KEY ("updated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_simulations_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "command_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each command type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the command type (e.g., Update, Edit) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of what this command type does */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "object_types" (
  "object_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each object type */,
  "object_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the object type (e.g., Simulation, Formulation) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the object type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("object_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "command_visibility" (
  "visibility_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each visibility setting */,
  "command_id" TEXT /* ID of the command associated with this visibility setting */,
  "is_visible" INTEGER /* Indicates if the command is visible in the user interface */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("visibility_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_command_visibility_command_id FOREIGN KEY ("command_id") REFERENCES commands ("command_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "command_comments" (
  "comment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comment */,
  "command_id" TEXT /* ID of the command the comment is associated with */,
  "comment" TEXT /* The comment text describing the command */,
  "created_by" TEXT /* User who created the comment */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the comment was created */,
  "updated_by" TEXT /* User who last updated the comment */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the comment was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_command_comments_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_command_comments_command_id FOREIGN KEY ("command_id") REFERENCES commands ("command_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_command_comments_updated_by FOREIGN KEY ("updated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "parent_commands" (
  "parent_command_id" TEXT /* ID of the parent command */,
  "child_command_id" TEXT /* ID of the child command that is related to the parent */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("parent_command_id", "child_command_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "command_sequence" (
  "sequence_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sequence entry */,
  "command_id" TEXT /* ID of the command in this sequence */,
  "sequence_order" INTEGER /* Order of this command in the sequence */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sequence_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_command_sequence_command_id FOREIGN KEY ("command_id") REFERENCES commands ("command_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "command_id" TEXT /* ID of the command that was changed */,
  "change_description" TEXT /* Description of what change was made */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date when the change was made */,
  "created_by" TEXT /* User who made the change */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the change was made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_command_id FOREIGN KEY ("command_id") REFERENCES commands ("command_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, admin) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was last updated */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user account (e.g., active, suspended, deleted) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
